title: ADR Frontmatter
description: YAML frontmatter for an ADR markdown file. Captures identity, lifecycle, relations and applicability. Executable rules live in a sibling .rules.yaml file referenced by 'rules_file'.

id.description: Stable identifier. Never renumber after merge.

title.description: Decision-stating headline. Sentence case. Avoid 'should' / 'we want' — state the decision.

status.description: Lifecycle state. 'draft' is pre-proposal. 'experimental' means accepted-but-revocable: rules may apply with severity capped at 'warning'. 'void' means the ADR slot was misclassified — content moved to the correct doc type (plan/review/etc.), ADR number retained for reference integrity.

status_history.description: Audit trail of status changes. Append-only.

decision_date.description: When the decision was made (calendar date, not commit time).

amended.description: Append-only list of substantive revisions to this ADR after first proposal. An amendment changes content but not identity — distinct from supersession (which replaces the ADR) and from status transitions (which change lifecycle). Use for clarifications, factual corrections, scope refinements.

decision_drivers.description: Structured forces driving the decision. Made explicit so audits can verify that supersessions actually address the original drivers.

open_questions.description: Known unanswered questions at decision time. Each has an owner and a deadline. Treated as first-class debt — surfaces in audit reports and in the constitution graph.

deprecation_timeline.description: When this ADR supersedes others, the timeline phases the transition. Critical for 'zero breaking changes' migrations: each phase says which version of the world is acceptable and what triggers the next phase.

spof_mitigation.description: Single-points-of-failure introduced by this decision and how they are mitigated. Required reading for production-impacting ADRs; flagged by audit when missing.

valid_from.description: Bi-temporal: when the decision takes effect in the world. Defaults to decision_date midnight UTC if absent.

valid_to.description: When the decision stops applying. null means open-ended. Set when status becomes 'superseded' or 'deprecated'.

knowledge_from.description: Bi-temporal: when WE LEARNED this should be the rule. Often equal to valid_from, but differs for retroactive rules.

retroactive.description: If true, rules apply to code committed before valid_from. Use sparingly, document reason in 'consequences'.

deciders.description: Humans (or principal:human pairs) who own this decision. Format: 'name <email>' or 'github:handle'.

domains.description: Slash-namespaced domain tags. Examples: 'django/models', 'celery', 'i18n', 'security/auth'. Used to filter rule applicability.

tech_stack.description: Free-form technology tags: 'python>=3.12', 'postgres>=16', 'django>=5.0'. Plain version constraints accepted.

supersedes.description: ADRs this decision replaces. Must be matched by 'superseded_by' on the target.

superseded_by.description: ADRs that replace this decision. Set automatically when target ADR is created with 'supersedes'.

depends_on.description: ADRs whose decisions this one assumes. Audit will flag dangling deps.

conflicts_with.description: Known conflicts. Audit may add more if it detects them.

informs.description: Weaker than depends_on: 'this is good context for understanding X'.

rules_file.description: Path to sibling .rules.yaml file with executable rules. null = informational ADR with no automatable rules.

tags.description: Free-form tags for search and filtering.

incident_links.description: Real incidents that motivated or stress-tested this decision. Critical for institutional memory.

last_reviewed.description: Last time a human confirmed this ADR is still correct.

updated.description: Most recent substantive update date. Distinct from amended[].at (which tracks specific amendments). Used for sort/filter operations. Real-world frequency: ~5%.

version.description: Revision counter — increments on substantive change. Distinct from amended[].version (which uses semver strings). Real-world frequency: ~3%.

review_status.description: Governance workflow state. Orthogonal to lifecycle 'status'. Tracks whether this ADR has been reviewed regardless of its lifecycle phase. Real-world frequency: ~3%.

owner.description: Single accountable person carrying operational responsibility. Distinct from deciders (plural decision-makers) and consulted (advisors). Receives `author` alias mappings. Real-world frequency: ~1% direct + ~3% via author alias.

implementation_done_when.description: Acceptance criteria for considering implementation complete. Should be testable. Read by adr_audit's implementation_evidence_check (Iter 3+). Real-world frequency: ~1%.

implementation_evidence.description: Concrete pointers to the implementation: commit SHAs, PR URLs, file paths, deployment IDs. Read by adr_audit to verify implementation_status is backed by evidence. Real-world frequency: ~62%.

related.description: Bidirectional 'see also' link to related ADRs. Weaker than depends_on (which implies a hard dependency). Audit does NOT enforce reciprocity (unlike supersedes/superseded_by). Real-world frequency: ~41%.

amends.description: 'This ADR amends X' — flat list of ADR-NNNN references whose decisions this one modifies without superseding. Distinct from the existing nested 'amended' field (which tracks revisions of THIS ADR). Real-world frequency: ~34%.

principal.description: Who owns this rule in the principal hierarchy. 'platform' rules apply repo-wide; 'operator' to a specific deployment; 'user' to per-user config.

repo.description: Origin repository of this ADR (the repo where it was first authored). null for repo-agnostic ADRs.

consumers.description: Downstream repositories that depend on or implement this ADR. Used by the constitution graph to compute blast-radius for changes.

consolidates.description: ADRs (or specific sections of ADRs) merged into this one. Stronger than 'supersedes' — implies that the consolidating ADR is the authoritative replacement for ALL of the consolidated decisions taken together. Each consolidated ADR's status SHOULD be set to 'superseded' with superseded_by pointing here.

implementation_status.description: Orthogonal to 'status'. A decision can be 'accepted' but 'implementation_status: none' (just decided, not built yet). Critical for distinguishing spec-drift from implementation-lag during audits.

per_repo_status.description: In a polyrepo platform an ADR often has different states across repos. Map of repo-name to per-repo lifecycle. If absent, the top-level 'status' / 'implementation_status' apply uniformly. Repo names should match those listed in 'consumers' or 'repo'.

planned_phase.description: Roadmap phase identifier (e.g. 'Phase 1', '2', or a date). Free-form to match existing roadmap conventions.

staleness_months.description: How many months without a review the ADR is considered stale. If absent, falls back to derivation from 'review_cadence'. Used to compute 'next_review_date' and to flag ADRs in audits.

drift_check_paths.description: Glob patterns telling auditors WHERE to look for drift against this ADR. Optimization: skips repo-wide scan. Examples: 'mcp-hub/rag_mcp/db/schema.sql', 'platform/packages/platform-search/**'. Differs from 'scope.include_paths' which constrains rule applicability — these paths are hints, scope is enforcement.

rationale_summary.description: Single-paragraph 'why'. Full rationale lives in the markdown body.

doc_type.description: Schema v4: Document type discriminator. Files in docs/adr/ MUST have doc_type: adr. Prevents misclassification of plans/reviews as ADRs (see ADR-181/182 incident).

reviewed_by.description: Schema v4: Human reviewers only. AI tools MUST NOT appear here — use ai_sparring_by instead. Empty = Bus Factor visible. WARNING when status=accepted and empty (Karenzzeit until 2026-11-10).

ai_sparring_by.description: Schema v4: AI tool contributions. Non-accountable — does NOT satisfy reviewed_by. Bus Factor=1 if reviewed_by is empty regardless of ai_sparring_by content.

external_sparring_by.description: Schema v4: External, cross-provider adversarial second opinion(s) via the /adr-handoff-extern flow. Distinct from ai_sparring_by (internal AI tool contributions during drafting): these are deliberately DIFFERENT-provider reviews used as a governance step before 'accepted'. Non-accountable — does NOT satisfy reviewed_by. Accepts the compact string form '<provider>@<YYYY-MM-DD>' (as written by the skill) or a structured array.

metrics.description: Schema v4: Auto-computed by adrfw nightly. Do NOT edit manually.

oneOf.description: Plain reference. Optional '§<section>' suffix for partial reference (e.g. 'ADR-187§E3' = section E3 of ADR-187).

id.description: ADR id without section suffix; use 'section' field instead for clarity.

section.description: Optional section identifier within the referenced ADR (e.g. 'E3', '4.2'). Lets one ADR partially supersede or consolidate part of another.

ADRRef.description: Reference to another ADR or part of one. Plain string for simple cases ('ADR-099') or section-scoped ('ADR-187§E3'). Object form when reason or scope must be attached.

evidence.description: Links to incidents, PRs, discussions backing the transition.

Scope.description: Limits where rules apply. All filters AND-combined. Empty object = repo-wide.

include_paths.description: Glob patterns of paths where rules apply. Default: all.

exclude_paths.description: Glob patterns to exclude. Common: 'tests/**', 'migrations/**' for some rules.

file_types.description: File extensions: '.py', '.html', '.yaml'. Default: all known types.

version.description: Semantic version of the amendment. Bump minor for clarifications, major for breaking changes that should arguably be a new ADR.

summary.description: One-line summary of what the amendment changed.

sections_changed.description: Section identifiers (e.g. 'E6', '3.', '8.') that were added or substantively modified.

rationale.description: Why the amendment was necessary. Often references review findings, audit results, or external feedback.

id.description: Local identifier within this ADR (D-1, D-2, ...).

driver.description: Concise statement of the force driving the decision.

weight.description: How strongly this driver shaped the outcome. Used by audit to verify supersession logic.

category.description: Category of concern. Optional but useful for cross-ADR analysis.

id.description: Local identifier within this ADR (Q-1, Q-2, ...).

decide_by.description: Deadline. Free-form to allow phase references like 'Phase 1' as well as ISO dates.

owner.description: Person accountable for resolving the question.

resolution.description: Filled in when status becomes 'resolved' or 'dropped'.

phase.description: Phase identifier, e.g. 'D-1' for the first deprecation step.

state.description: World state during this phase — what is acceptable, what is not.

earliest.description: Earliest the phase can begin. May reference another phase ('Phase 1 done') or be a date.

completion_signal.description: Concrete signal that this phase is complete and the next can begin.

component.description: What is the SPOF: a database, service, vendor, secret, ...

phase.description: Roadmap phase when this mitigation is in place.

HumanReview.description: Schema v4: A human's formal review. AI tool names MUST NOT appear in the 'name' field.

name.description: Human identifier: full name, github handle, or email.

AISparring.description: Schema v4: AI tool contribution. Non-accountable — does NOT satisfy reviewed_by requirement.

ExternalSparring.description: Schema v4: One external, cross-provider adversarial review obtained via /adr-handoff-extern. Non-accountable — does NOT satisfy reviewed_by.

provider.description: External model/provider, e.g. 'openai/o3', 'gemini', or 'two-external-llms' when aggregated.

verdict.description: Overall recommendation of the external review.

findings_ref.description: Pointer to the tag-table / findings (ADR body section anchor or ~/shared briefing path).
